shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "next.js server action cache tag products catalog sync" --json --top-k 5'

/bin/bash -lc 'mkdir -p "$HOME/.mempal-notes" && cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/next-products-tag-background-sync.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Use products cache tag with background revalidation for admin catalog syncs

For read-heavy Next.js admin catalog pages, cache the shared catalog read behind "'`use cache` plus `cacheTag('"'products')"'`, then trigger upstream sync work from a Server Action without awaiting the full recompute. After the background sync finishes, call `revalidateTag('"'products', 'max')"'` so requests stay responsive and views sharing the `products` tag converge shortly after via stale-while-revalidate semantics instead of blocking on fresh data.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'

errors: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
